title: triticum tauri
intro: Triticum tauri, also known as wild emmer, is an ancient, extinct species of wheat that was once native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region. It is believed to be a precursor to modern emmer wheat and holds potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Triticum tauri include its ability to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce the risk of certain diseases due to its high fiber and nutrient content. Triticum tauri can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its uses and effects.
